claimText: SEBI is a statutory body established under the SEBI Act of 1992 and possesses civil court powers for enforcement.
evidenceExcerpt: SEBI became a statutory body after the SEBI Act of 1992 and possesses civil court powers under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal status and powers of SEBI.

claimText: The SEBI Board includes a chairman, two members from the Finance Ministry, one member from the RBI, and five nominees from the Union Government.
evidenceExcerpt: The SEBI Board includes a chairman, two Finance Ministry members, one RBI member, and five Union Government nominees.
examUse: Statement-based question on the composition of the SEBI Board.

claimText: The SWAGAT-FI framework was introduced by SEBI for Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) and Foreign Venture Capital Investors (FVCIs).
evidenceExcerpt: The SWAGAT-FI framework was introduced for FPIs and Foreign Venture Capital Investors (FVCIs).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching regulatory frameworks to their target investor groups.

claimText: Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) invest in income-producing real estate, whereas Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs) invest in infrastructure assets like roads and power lines.
evidenceExcerpt: REITs allow investment in income-producing real estate, while InvITs invest in infrastructure assets like roads and power lines.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing the investment focus of REITs and InvITs.
distractorHooks: InvITs invest in commercial real estate
